items.title: Lowfat: Pluggable CLI filter that saves up to 91% of LLM tokens
items.why_it_matters: This open-source CLI tool filters out verbose terminal outputs (like massive database dumps or build logs) before passing them to your LLM agent. Since you use Claude Code daily, stripping this noise directly reduces your token usage and keeps your API costs low. This week, install the binary and try piping your Next.js build or Supabase CLI commands through it to see how much terminal noise you can filter out.

items.title: Agents Remember: Git-aware memory for coding agents
items.why_it_matters: This tool creates a Markdown-based, Git-tracked memory ledger that helps coding agents maintain context of your repository's architecture across sessions. Since you are practicing strict commit discipline on your AI-ingest repo, this is a perfect way to keep Claude Code aligned with your codebase as it grows. Try initializing a basic memory file in your AI-ingest project this week to help Claude track your project structure.

items.title: Customize keyboard shortcuts in Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has documented how to customize keybindings and keyboard shortcuts directly within Claude Code. Because Claude Code is your primary environment where you direct the AI, optimizing your shortcuts can significantly speed up your daily build loops. Open your Claude Code configuration file today and bind a custom shortcut for `/commit` to make saving your progress even faster.

items.title: Miasma Worm targets AI coding agents via GitHub repos
items.why_it_matters: A new security threat has emerged where malicious configurations are embedded in public GitHub repositories, designed to execute arbitrary code when an AI agent runs inside that directory. Since you use Claude Code locally on your machine, running it inside unverified cloned repos can put your local environment and Supabase API keys at risk. Make it a habit this week to manually inspect hidden config files like `.claudecode` before starting an agent session in a newly cloned repo.
